---
title: "CGIAR Climate Data Hub - System-Wide Climate Data Asset Mapping"
subtitle: "Internal review draft"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: false
    embed-resources: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        #TOC {
          position: sticky;
          top: 1rem;
          max-height: calc(100vh - 2rem);
          overflow-y: auto;
        }
        @media print {
          details.code-fold summary,
          details.code-fold .sourceCode,
          details.code-fold .cell-output {
            display: none !important;
          }
        }
        /* Tables: smaller type, tight padding, columns sized to content so the
           last (definition / rationale) column gets the remaining width. */
        main table, .cell table, table {
          font-size: 0.80rem;
          width: 100%;
          table-layout: auto;
          border-collapse: collapse;
        }
        main table th, main table td,
        table th, table td {
          padding: 0.28rem 0.5rem;
          vertical-align: top;
          line-height: 1.3;
        }
        /* Collapse the leading label/numeric column (Domain, Centre, #, Rank)
           to its content width, leaving the rest for the wide text column. */
        table td:first-child, table th:first-child {
          white-space: nowrap;
          width: 1%;
        }
        /* The wide text column should wrap and absorb remaining width. */
        table td:last-child, table th:last-child {
          white-space: normal;
          width: auto;
        }
        /* Figure captions: tighter, smaller, secondary to the plot. */
        figure figcaption, .figure-caption, p > img + em {
          font-size: 0.82rem;
          color: #444;
          line-height: 1.35;
        }
        </style>
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: false
  docx:
    toc: true
    number-sections: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"
import pathlib
import sys
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

ROOT = pathlib.Path.cwd()
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / "src"))

# Set live dashboard link here when official endpoint is available.
DASHBOARD_URL = "docs/index.html"
DASHBOARD_LABEL = "CDH interactive dashboard"

from report_common import build_report_body, compute_stats, load
```

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"
assets, merge_log = load()
stats = compute_stats(assets, merge_log)
```

**Version:** 1.0-draft  
**Report month:** `{{< meta date >}}`  
**Status:** Internal review draft  
**Assets in current build:** `{python} stats["total"]`

```{python}
#| output: asis
if DASHBOARD_URL:
    display(Markdown(
        f"**Interactive data access:** [{DASHBOARD_LABEL}]({DASHBOARD_URL})"
    ))
else:
    display(Markdown(
        "**Interactive data access:** static dashboard available at `docs/index.html`."
    ))
```

```{python}
#| label: report-body
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show the code that generates this report"
#| output: asis
# Every section, statistic, and table below is generated from
# data/normalized/assets.json by build_report_body() in src/report_common.py.
# No numbers are hand-typed. The full pipeline (Excel -> assets.json -> figures
# -> this report) is documented in Section 2.4 and version-controlled at
# https://github.com/CGIAR-Climate-Data-Hub/cdh-asset-mapping
display(Markdown(build_report_body(assets, stats, figures_prefix="outputs/figures")))
```
